reviewsSample.text:
The employees' treatment of customers is very bad. Most of the employees are grimacing and arrogant, with their faces up to the sky as if they are working at NASA, especially those who work in the spices and nuts department. Do they think they are giving us products from their own pockets for free? Don't they know that we pay thousands of dinars to pay their own salaries? If customers stop coming to this market, the market will close sooner or later. Therefore they need to be nice with customers.

And if you make an order, it takes about six hours for the order to arrive. The reason is that they don’t care about customers at all. They are greedy and they care about money only.

Nice and useful for shopping; there is only one problem which is the small space and narrow walking passages; I hope the owner enlarging the place or move to a bigger one.

Important Update 2023/
It looks much better after moving to the new location with one more floor and a restaurant.

The prices are relatively fair.

The goods are good.

Goods Variety is covering the essential needs for the family.

The service is good.
My favorite supermarket,and their fast food is really good

Their treatment is very bad and they have no professionalism or knowledge in dealing with people. They do not greet or respond to greeting  or even say thank you to the customers. It's as if we came to take things for free.. They always put the purchases in one bag only. They mix everything together and each type is supposed to be isolated because there are things that do not mix together, but they do not understand. I don't know, maybe their manager does not understand anything about market procedures. I mean, he needs professional training.

The service is very bad. When you order something from them they take a long time to respond and complete the order. You have to wait for more than two hours to receive your order. And worse than that they will send you rotten and bad fruits and vegetables. In addition to that, most of their products are very expensive too. I don't recommend this place for shopping.

reviewsSample.text:
Historical place, it is small and not good for hanging out. You can go there to take some memorial photos.
I recommend to the authorities to add the free wifi service cause it will be helpful fir some people who may be in an emergency situation. Generally, I liked this place regarding my comments above which were constructive ones.
It’s a very nice place but lots of rubbish everywhere it needs cleaning, The statue is very nice but they should open the fountain, time to time the water seems to stop working

for the most part its great but not that much content is to be said about it, but there's four commercial streets connected to it that all have different kinds of stuff such as private doctors, jewellery shops, hotels or general house shopping.
The buzzing downtown of Nasiriyah city. Shops, cafes, and small restaurants are all around this central square. The best time to visit is afternoon and evening time 🌃

Al-Haboubi Square in Nasiriyah is a key cultural and social landmark, named after poet Mohammed Saeed Al-Haboubi. It features his statue and is a hub for gatherings, protests, and daily life. Surrounded by shops and cafes, it holds historical and political significance but could benefit from better maintenance.

College student

The central markets built during Saddam's era are now occupied by the College of Science, and a small building was constructed on part of it to house the College of Computer Science and Mathematics. The space is very small and overcrowded, with a large number of students and faculty members. There is very little seating available for students outside the classrooms, a negligible percentage of the total student population.

It's a beautiful place, but it needs expansion. It also has several departments, including medical analysis, earth science, chemistry, biology, and physics. It's affiliated with Thi Qar University and located near the Nasiriyah Municipal Park.
